content:
# Igneous and Metamorphic Petrology

**Summary**: Broad research theme addressed in the thesis collection.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1993-kaka|Kaka, 1993]] contributed to the theme of Igneous Metamorphic Petrology

- [[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023]] applied igneous-metamorphic-petrology to characterize mantle xenoliths.

- [[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024]] analyzed the petrogenesis and mineralogy of granitic rocks.

- [[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024]] focused on the igneous-metamorphic-petrology of the Al-Ghurayyah pluton.

## Key Subtopics



- [[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023]] applied igneous-metamorphic-petrology to characterize mantle xenoliths.

- [[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024]] analyzed the petrogenesis and mineralogy of granitic rocks.

- [[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024]] focused on the igneous-metamorphic-petrology of the Al-Ghurayyah pluton.

## Methods and Approaches Used



## Key Findings Across Sources

- contributed to the theme of Igneous Metamorphic Petrology ([[1993-kaka|Kaka, 1993]])

- Harrat Uwayrid peridotite xenoliths are classified as spinel lherzolite and spinel harzburgite. ([[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023, p. 21]])

- Granitoids in the Arabian Shield show a diversity in their geochemical, tectonic, and origin characteristics. ([[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024, p. 21]])

- Identified three distinct types of pegmatites with different mineral zonation patterns. ([[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024, p. 41]])
